ancelInteractiveConsole extends the jython InteractiveConsole to override methods allowing it to handle CancelException while running the interact() method. It runs the read requests in a separate thread so that the calling thread can cancel. Wh

thread so that the calling thread can cancel. While this is an added complexity, it is necessary because it is not normally possible to cancel a request to read the input once it is started. Fields inherited from class org.python.util.Interactive

wait, wait Constructor Detail CancelInteractiveConsole public CancelInteractiveConsole () Creates new CancelInteractiveConsole. This just calls super(). Method Detail flushReader public void flushReader () throws CancelException Th

ader () throws CancelException This can be called before interact() to make sure any previous lines in the input buffer are cleared. This is useful because if the user clicked cancel the previous time while a line was being typed,

el the previous time while a line was being typed, we don't want it included in the next interactive session. This calls CancelThread.check() to maybe throw a CancelException. Throws: CancelException - thrown by CancelThread.check() See Also: Cance

n - thrown by CancelThread.check() See Also: CancelThread raw_input public java.lang.String raw_input (org.python.core.PyObject prompt) Write a prompt and read a line. When the user enters "exit" or the input stream ends, EOFError is raised. If can

the input stream ends, EOFError is raised. If cancelThread.check() throws an exception, this raises it as a PyException. This overrides raw_input in InteractiveConsole. Overrides: raw_input in class org.python.util.InteractiveConsole Parameters: p

s org.python.util.InteractiveConsole Parameters: prompt - the prompt to print for reading the line Returns: the line read from the input. The returned line does not include the trailing newline. Throws: org.python.core.PyException - For th

line. Throws: org.python.core.PyException - For the end of input, the type of exception is Py.EOFError, which will be caught by the interact() method causing it to exit. For a cancel returned from CancelThread.check(),

cel returned from CancelThread.check(), this converts the exception to a PyException with Py.JavaError, which will be re-thrown by interact(). runcode public void runcode (org.python.core.PyObject code) Execute a code object. All

on.core.PyObject code) Execute a code object. All exceptions are caught and simply displayed with showexception() except Py.SystemExit and a PyException version of CancelException, which are re-raised. This overrides InteractiveCompiler.runcode()
